special_mechanics.name: Object Pushing
special_mechanics.description: Player can push brick blocks, barrels, candles, and elevator controlling blocks to navigate rooms and progress. Movement is restricted and deliberate.

physics_values.push_mechanics: Objects must be precisely aligned to open doors
physics_values.movement_constraint: Cannot open doors while jumping mid-air

special_mechanics.name: Sword Attack
special_mechanics.description: Prince can attack enemies with sword/dagger in NES/Famicom versions only. Attack requires close proximity and precise timing. Enemy must be very close for attack to connect.

special_mechanics.name: Dash
special_mechanics.description: Prince can dash and retrieve weapon on timely basis. Poor timing can prevent launching another attack.

tier_notes: Tier 3 (Empirical/Qualitative) - No precise physics values documented in available sources. Sources contain only gameplay descriptions, regional differences, and critical reception. Movement is described as 'slow' by Nintendo Power. Game emphasizes logic and puzzle-solving over action mechanics.
